title: Rise of Nazi Germany
summary: How a defeated, humiliated democracy collapsed into dictatorship — and how the world let Hitler march toward another war.

context.headline: A continent that would not say no
context.description: Beaten in 1918 and punished at Versailles, Germany became a fragile democracy that broke under depression and despair. Adolf Hitler rose by promising to undo that humiliation. Step by step he rebuilt Germany's army and swallowed his neighbours — the Rhineland, Austria, Czechoslovakia, then Poland — while Britain and France, desperate to avoid another war, did almost nothing to stop him.
